comment_text.sn24.5:1.3: This nihilist doctrine (natthikavāda) is attributed to Ajita of the Hair Blanket at DN 2:23.2. | The denial of “mother and father” is usually interpreted as the denial of moral duty towards one’s parents. However, I think it is a doctrine of conception which denies that a child is created by the mother and father. Rather, the child is produced by the four elements, with parents as mere instigators and incubators. | Compare Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad 4.3.23 on the liberated Self, “A father is not a father, a mother is not a mother, worlds are not worlds, gods are not gods, and Vedas are not Vedas. Here a thief is not a thief, an abortionist is not an abortionist, an outcaste is not an outcaste, a pariah is not a pariah, a recluse is not a recluse, and an ascetic is not an ascetic. Neither the good nor the bad follows him, for he has now passed beyond all sorrows of the heart.”
comment_text.sn24.5:1.10: Dattu is a rare term for “idiot” or “moron”, only spoken by sectarian teachers, so should probably be distinguished from the Buddhist “fool”.

root_text.sn24.5:1.2: “Kismiṁ nu kho, bhikkhave, sati kiṁ upādāya, kiṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.5:1.3: ‘natthi dinnaṁ, natthi yiṭṭhaṁ, natthi hutaṁ, natthi sukatadukkaṭānaṁ kammānaṁ phalaṁ vipāko; natthi ayaṁ loko, natthi paro loko, natthi mātā, natthi pitā, natthi sattā opapātikā; natthi loke samaṇabrāhmaṇā sammaggatā sammāpaṭipannā ye imañca lokaṁ parañca lokaṁ sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā pavedenti.
root_text.sn24.5:1.4: Cātumahābhūtiko ayaṁ puriso yadā kālaṁ karoti pathavī pathavīkāyaṁ anupeti anupagacchati, āpo āpokāyaṁ anupeti anupagacchati, tejo tejokāyaṁ anupeti anupagacchati, vāyo vāyokāyaṁ anupeti anupagacchati.
root_text.sn24.5:1.11: Tesaṁ tucchaṁ musā vilāpo ye keci atthikavādaṁ vadanti.
root_text.sn24.5:1.12: Bāle ca paṇḍite ca kāyassa bhedā ucchijjanti vinassanti na honti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.5:2.1: “Rūpe kho, bhikkhave, sati, rūpaṁ upādāya, rūpaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.5:2.3: kāyassa bhedā ucchijjanti vinassanti na honti paraṁ maraṇā’ti.
root_text.sn24.5:2.7: viññāṇe sati, viññāṇaṁ upādāya, viññāṇaṁ abhinivissa evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjati:
root_text.sn24.5:2.9: kāyassa bhedā ucchijjanti vinassanti na honti paraṁ maraṇā’ti.
root_text.sn24.5:3.4: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.5:3.6: kāyassa bhedā ucchijjanti vinassanti na honti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.5:3.13: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.5:3.15: kāyassa bhedā ucchijjanti vinassanti na honti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.5:3.17: “Yampidaṁ diṭṭhaṁ sutaṁ mutaṁ viññātaṁ pattaṁ pariyesitaṁ anuvicaritaṁ manasā tampi niccaṁ vā aniccaṁ vā”ti?
root_text.sn24.5:3.19: api nu taṁ anupādāya evaṁ diṭṭhi uppajjeyya:
root_text.sn24.5:3.22: bāle ca paṇḍite ca kāyassa bhedā ucchijjanti vinassanti na honti paraṁ maraṇā’”ti?
root_text.sn24.5:4.1: “Yato kho, bhikkhave, ariyasāvakassa imesu ca ṭhānesu kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti, dukkhepissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti …pe… dukkhanirodhagāminiyā paṭipadāyapissa kaṅkhā pahīnā hoti—
root_text.sn24.5:4.2: ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako sotāpanno avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyano”ti.

translation_text.sn24.5:1.2: “Mendicants, when what exists, because of grasping what and insisting on what, does the view arise:
translation_text.sn24.5:1.3: ‘There’s no meaning in giving, sacrifice, or offerings. There’s no fruit or result of good and bad deeds. There’s no afterlife. There’s no such thing as mother and father, or beings that are reborn spontaneously. And there’s no ascetic or brahmin who is rightly comported and rightly practiced, and who describes the afterlife after realizing it with their own insight.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.4: This person is made up of the four principal states. When they die, the earth in their body progresses and proceeds to the substance of earth. The water in their body progresses and proceeds to the substance of water. The fire in their body progresses and proceeds to the substance of fire. The air in their body progresses and proceeds to the substance of air.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.5: The faculties are transferred to space.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.6: Four men with a bier carry away the corpse.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.7: Their footprints show the way to the cremation ground.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.9: Offerings dedicated to the gods end in ashes.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.10: Giving is a doctrine of morons.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.11: When anyone affirms a positive teaching it’s just baseless, false nonsense.
translation_text.sn24.5:1.12: Both the foolish and the astute are annihilated and destroyed when their body breaks up, and don’t exist after death’?”
translation_text.sn24.5:1.13: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. …”
translation_text.sn24.5:2.1: “When form exists, because of grasping form and insisting on form, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.5:2.2: ‘There’s no meaning in giving, sacrifice, or offerings. …
translation_text.sn24.5:2.3: Both the foolish and the astute are annihilated and destroyed when their body breaks up, and don’t exist after death.’
translation_text.sn24.5:2.7: consciousness exists, because of grasping consciousness and insisting on consciousness, the view arises:
translation_text.sn24.5:2.8: ‘There’s no meaning in giving, sacrifice, or offerings. …
translation_text.sn24.5:2.9: Both the foolish and the astute are annihilated and destroyed when their body breaks up, and don’t exist after death.’
translation_text.sn24.5:3.17: “That which is seen, heard, thought, known, attained, sought, and explored by the mind: is that permanent or impermanent?”
translation_text.sn24.5:3.19: “But by not grasping what’s impermanent, suffering, and perishable, would such a view arise?”
translation_text.sn24.5:4.1: “When a noble disciple has given up doubt regarding these six grounds, and has given up doubt in suffering, its origin, its cessation, and the practice that leads to its cessation,
translation_text.sn24.5:4.2: they’re called a noble disciple who is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”

variant_text.sn24.5:1.4: Cātumahābhūtiko ayaṁ → cātummahābhūtiko ayaṁ (bj, km); cātummahābhūtikoyaṁ (sya-all); catummahābhūtiko (pts1ed)
variant_text.sn24.5:1.10: Dattupaññattaṁ yadidaṁ dānaṁ → dattupaññattamidaṁ dānaṁ nāma (sabbattha)
